# Portal news views broken: deep-link tab activation (discussions/pulls), PR tree structure ≠ tickets, PR theming (avatars/timeline)

## Operator-reported, live on dev (portal-news GOAL views #12211/#12213)

Three runtime/visual defects in the merged portal "news" views. The PR view (#12213) and Discussions view (#12211) diverged from the working **tickets** view; the fix is to mirror tickets.

### 1. Deep-link does not activate the correct tab (discussions + pulls)
Loading `#/news/discussions/<id>` or `#/news/pulls/<id>` does NOT activate the tab on initial load — only manual selection works. Tickets/Releases work because their route handlers set a **hardcoded** `activeIndex`; discussions/pulls call `setActiveIndexByRoute()` → `items.findIndex(i => i.header?.route === route)`, which returns -1 on initial load (items/headers not yet resolved when the route fires). Fix: hardcoded indices matching the tab order (discussions=4, pulls=5), mirroring the working tickets(2)/releases(3) handlers.

### 2. PR view tree JSON structure ≠ tickets
`pulls.json` is chunk-folder-based (`childrenUrl` lazy chunks) while `tickets.json` is a flat tree (leaves with `path` directly under state-group roots). The inconsistency is wrong — pulls should mirror the tickets flat shape. (The lazy/chunked migration, if wanted, must be done consistently for BOTH views, tracked separately as #12219 — not pulls-only.)

### 3. PR view theming broken (avatars full-size, timeline overlaps content)
`pulls/Component.mjs` emits different CSS classes / DOM than `tickets/Component.mjs`, so the timeline+avatar styling doesn't apply → full-size profile images + timeline overlapping content. Fix: align the pulls DOM classes (e.g. `.neo-timeline-item.comment`) + ensure the timeline/avatar SCSS applies to the pulls view (share or mirror the tickets styles).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deep-link to `#/news/discussions/<id>` and `#/news/pulls/<id>` activates the correct tab on initial load.
- [ ] `pulls.json` tree structure matches the `tickets.json` shape.
- [ ] PR view renders with correctly-sized avatars + a non-overlapping timeline (visually matches the tickets view).

## Root context
The pulls view shipped without L3 (in-browser) verification (PR #12287 evidence noted L3 as residual). Mirror the tickets view across structure + theming + routing.

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● P0 coordination note from GPT lead lane.
> 
> Operator escalated this as the main GOAL epic recovery item: portal app is seriously broken after the merged portal-news changes.
> 
> Neural Link evidence from live Portal worker `b6259b26-55dd-438f-9636-2380f79a1877`, window `efa66204-f249-4c0b-8d61-3a557b83cffb`:
> - Current route history includes `/news/pulls/12099` and captured route `/news/pulls/{*itemId}`.
> - Component tree contains `Portal.view.news.pulls.MainContainer` (`neo-container-4`) and `Portal.view.news.pulls.Component` (`neo-markdown-1`).
> - `neo-tab-container-1` reports `activeIndex: null` after the deep-link path.
> - Pulls header button (`neo-tab-header-button-6`) has `text: Pull Requests`, `route: /news/pulls`, `pressed: true`.
> - Actual content activation is wrong: `Portal.view.news.pulls.MainContainer` has `mounted: false`, `Neo.tab.BodyContainer` has `mounted: false`, while `Portal.view.home.MainContainer` has `mounted: true`.
> - DOM rects for pulls content components are all zero, consistent with mounted/visible route state divergence.
> - No App Worker console errors were present, so this is a routing/body activation/state-shape regression, not a thrown runtime exception.
> 
> Coordination split:
> - Claude keeps the author lane for #12300 since the ticket is already assigned there.
> - GPT will take verifier/reviewer lane and run NL/L3 checks on the fix PR.
> - I added `regression` + `QA` labels because the repo has no exact P0 label.
> 
> Do not treat the tree-shape issue as an invitation to resurrect pulls-only lazy chunking. The ticket’s constraint is to mirror tickets now; chunked migration belongs to the consistent tickets+pulls perf lane (#12219).

- 2026-06-01T09:37:55Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12301
- 2026-06-01T09:46:02Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `59da9c4` - "fix(portal): flat pulls.json matching the tickets tree shape (#12300)

The PR tree shipped chunked (group → chunk-folder → lazy leaf files) while tickets.json is flat
(group roots → PR leaves with path). Rewrote buildScripts/docs/index/pulls.mjs to emit the flat
shape mirroring tickets.mjs (group roots + PR leaves carrying their markdown path), regenerated
pulls.json (3139 flat nodes, no childrenUrl), and removed the stale per-chunk leaf files. The PR
MainContainer treeConfig now matches tickets exactly (lazyChildLoad stays for parity — inert on a
flat tree, both views go chunked together later in #12219). Content loads via the shared TreeList
record.path, same as tickets."
- 2026-06-01T10:14:44Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `156bd10` - "test+docs(portal): align pulls generator test + store/model comments with the flat tree (#12300)

Per cross-family review: the flat-tree generator change left the contract mixed. Updated
PortalContentIndexes.spec.mjs to assert the flat shape (group roots + PR leaves with path, no
childrenUrl, no per-chunk dir) — was asserting the old chunk-folder output; and rewrote the
Portal.store.Pulls + Portal.model.Pull comments that still described chunked/lazy loading (the
chunk-folder model fields are retained in parity with the tickets model for the future surface)."
- 2026-06-01T12:18:12Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `9b196aa` - "fix(portal): release-group + chunk PR tree + share timeline CSS (#12300)

Corrects the portal Pull Requests view structure per operator direction and
addresses the cycle-2 review.

Tree structure: the PR index is now release-grouped (Latest for unreleased +
one group per release version, semver-desc) instead of the Merged/Open/Closed
split, which made no sense given ~99% of PRs are merged. pulls.mjs now mirrors
tickets.mjs: a flat legacy pulls.json plus the chunked/lazy surface
(pulls/index.json + per-chunk leaf JSONs + manifest.json), matching the on-disk
content/pulls + content/archive/pulls/<version> folder layout. Routes stay flat
(#/news/pulls/<number>). The lazy-consumer migration (store + deep-link chunk
resolution) is the shared follow-up tracked in #12219.

Timeline CSS: pulls/Component.scss now @use "../tickets/Component" (mirroring
discussions) so the shared timeline layout rules ship in pulls' per-component
CSS. Without it the timeline rendered unstyled (centered, avatars as full-width
images), because Neo lazy-loads theme CSS per concrete component.

Avatar: hoisted getAvatarUrl/getAvatarHtml to the shared
Portal.view.content.Component base (sized ?size=40 to bound network cost, plus a
Font Awesome GitHub glyph fallback for bot/[bot] actors whose avatar image 404s);
pulls inherits it. Added .neo-timeline-avatar-icon sizing.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-06-01T12:43:10Z @tobiu referenced in commit `341e7ec` - "fix(portal): deep-link tab activation + PR-view timeline theming (#12300) (#12301)

* fix(portal): activate discussions/pulls tab on deep-link via hardcoded index (#12300)

setActiveIndexByRoute() did items.findIndex(i => i.header?.route === route), which returns -1 on
initial deep-link (items/headers not resolved when the route fires) — so #/news/discussions/<id> and
#/news/pulls/<id> did not activate their tab on load (only manual selection worked). Tickets/Releases
worked because they set a hardcoded activeIndex. Mirror that proven pattern: discussions=4, pulls=5
(matching the news TabContainer items order); drop the init-unsafe setActiveIndexByRoute helper.

* fix(portal): apply shared timeline theming to the PR view (#12300)

The shared timeline/avatar/comment SCSS lives in tickets/Component.scss scoped to
.portal-news-tickets-component, .portal-news-discussions-component — the PR view's
.portal-news-pulls-component was omitted, so avatars rendered full-size and the timeline overlapped
content (the .neo-timeline-avatar { width/position } + .comment block never applied). Discussions
gets its timeline styling from this same shared scope (its own SCSS has none), so add pulls to it.
Also give every avatar-bearing PR bubble the .comment class (body + reviews, matching tickets'
body/comment items) so the .comment avatar/content layout applies to all of them.

* fix(portal): flat pulls.json matching the tickets tree shape (#12300)

The PR tree shipped chunked (group → chunk-folder → lazy leaf files) while tickets.json is flat
(group roots → PR leaves with path). Rewrote buildScripts/docs/index/pulls.mjs to emit the flat
shape mirroring tickets.mjs (group roots + PR leaves carrying their markdown path), regenerated
pulls.json (3139 flat nodes, no childrenUrl), and removed the stale per-chunk leaf files. The PR
MainContainer treeConfig now matches tickets exactly (lazyChildLoad stays for parity — inert on a
flat tree, both views go chunked together later in #12219). Content loads via the shared TreeList
record.path, same as tickets.

* test+docs(portal): align pulls generator test + store/model comments with the flat tree (#12300)

Per cross-family review: the flat-tree generator change left the contract mixed. Updated
PortalContentIndexes.spec.mjs to assert the flat shape (group roots + PR leaves with path, no
childrenUrl, no per-chunk dir) — was asserting the old chunk-folder output; and rewrote the
Portal.store.Pulls + Portal.model.Pull comments that still described chunked/lazy loading (the
chunk-folder model fields are retained in parity with the tickets model for the future surface).

* fix(portal): release-group + chunk PR tree + share timeline CSS (#12300)

Corrects the portal Pull Requests view structure per operator direction and
addresses the cycle-2 review.

Tree structure: the PR index is now release-grouped (Latest for unreleased +
one group per release version, semver-desc) instead of the Merged/Open/Closed
split, which made no sense given ~99% of PRs are merged. pulls.mjs now mirrors
tickets.mjs: a flat legacy pulls.json plus the chunked/lazy surface
(pulls/index.json + per-chunk leaf JSONs + manifest.json), matching the on-disk
content/pulls + content/archive/pulls/<version> folder layout. Routes stay flat
(#/news/pulls/<number>). The lazy-consumer migration (store + deep-link chunk
resolution) is the shared follow-up tracked in #12219.

Timeline CSS: pulls/Component.scss now @use "../tickets/Component" (mirroring
discussions) so the shared timeline layout rules ship in pulls' per-component
CSS. Without it the timeline rendered unstyled (centered, avatars as full-width
images), because Neo lazy-loads theme CSS per concrete component.

Avatar: hoisted getAvatarUrl/getAvatarHtml to the shared
Portal.view.content.Component base (sized ?size=40 to bound network cost, plus a
Font Awesome GitHub glyph fallback for bot/[bot] actors whose avatar image 404s);
pulls inherits it. Added .neo-timeline-avatar-icon sizing.
